creator: Russell Brandom
title: (No AI) OpenAI proposed donating 5% of its equity to a US sovereign wealth fund
dc:creator: Russell Brandom
content: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman has reportedly proposed giving 5% of the company’s equity to a U.S. sovereign wealth fund, reviving discussions about letting the public share in the financial gains from the AI boom.
contentSnippet: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman has reportedly proposed giving 5% of the company’s equity to a U.S. sovereign wealth fund, reviving discussions about letting the public share in the financial gains from the AI boom.

title: (No AI) OpenAI floats giving Trump administration 5 percent cut of AI boom
author: Robert Hart
content:
![](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/07/STK201_SAM_ALTMAN_CVIRGINIA2A.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

OpenAI has floated giving the US government a 5 percent ownership stake as a way of easing tensions with the Trump administration and blunting mounting public backlash against AI, [according to the _Financial Times_](https://www.ft.com/content/7c803eab-8e80-4431-9a87-e943bf00e00b?syn-25a6b1a6=1).

CEO Sam Altman argued that giving the public a financial interest in the company would be the best way to share the upside of AI, the _FT_ reported, citing two unnamed people familiar with the talks. He's said to have [first pitched the idea to Trump](https://www.theverge.com/policy/944302/sam-altman-reportedly-talked-to-the-trump-administration-about-taking-a-stake-in-openai) early last year.

Altman reportedly suggested the 5 percent figure. Based on OpenAI's [latest funding round](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/946335/openai-ipo-s-1-confidential), which ended with the company valued at $852 billion, that stake would be worth …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/960588/openai-government-5-percent-stake-trump)
contentSnippet:
OpenAI has floated giving the US government a 5 percent ownership stake as a way of easing tensions with the Trump administration and blunting mounting public backlash against AI, according to the Financial Times.
CEO Sam Altman argued that giving the public a financial interest in the company would be the best way to share the upside of AI, the FT reported, citing two unnamed people familiar with the talks. He's said to have first pitched the idea to Trump early last year.
Altman reportedly suggested the 5 percent figure. Based on OpenAI's latest funding round, which ended with the company valued at $852 billion, that stake would be worth …
Read the full story at The Verge.
summary: OpenAI has floated giving the US government a 5 percent ownership stake as a way of easing tensions with the Trump administration and blunting mounting public backlash against AI, according to the Financial Times. CEO Sam Altman argued that giving the public a financial interest in the company would be the best way to share […]

title: (No AI) Google built a great smart speaker, but Gemini isn’t ready for it
author: Jennifer Pattison Tuohy
content:
![](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/06/268614_Google_Home_Speaker_JTuohy_0009.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

The Google Home Speaker is Google’s first smart speaker in years. And it’s pretty! | Photo: Jennifer Patison Tuohy / The Verge

Smart speakers have spent the past few years searching for a compelling second act. Beyond music, timers, and controlling your lights, they've struggled to justify taking up space on the kitchen counter. [AI promised to change that](https://www.theverge.com/2024/6/14/24177991/apple-intelligence-siri-voice-assistant-amazon-alexa-generative-ai).

Amazon debuted its new [hardware powered by a revamped Alexa](https://www.theverge.com/tech/788051/hands-on-amazon-alexa-echo-show-11-echo-dot-max-echo-studio) last fall, and now it's finally Google's turn. The Google Home Speaker is the company's first new smart speaker in six years and [its first "built for Gemini."](https://www.theverge.com/tech/788102/gemini-for-home-new-google-assistant-launch-date-price-features) After years of neglect, Google appears to be finally getting serious about the smart home - again. The new speaker is the clearest sign yet. [Gemini for Home](https://www.theverge.com/news/762370/google-announces-gemini-for-home-nest-smart-speakers-voice-assistant), however, still feels unfinished.

### Google …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/tech/959503/google-home-speaker-review-gemini-for-home)

creator: Ej Dickson
title: (No AI) Goose, a New Gay Dating App, Appears to Be a Psyop
dc:creator: Ej Dickson
content: Touted as a less-hookup-focused Grindr, Goose is an invite-only space for gay men. The problem is the people promoting it don’t seem real.
contentSnippet: Touted as a less-hookup-focused Grindr, Goose is an invite-only space for gay men. The problem is the people promoting it don’t seem real.
